title: link
text: lnk_barrier_overrides(conn, barriers, observations, habitat, exclusions, control, params, ) — processes fish observations habitat confirmations barrier skip list fresh. Counts observations upstream barrier via fwa_upstream() SQL, applies per-species thresholds, unions habitat confirmations. Control table (barriers_definite_control barrier_ind = TRUE rows) blocks override flagged positions — gated per-species params$observation_control_apply residents (BT, WCT) can still override anadromous-blocking falls. Habitat path bypasses control entirely (expert-confirmed habitat higher-trust observations). Output: (blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, species_code) table fresh skips access gating.

title: link
text: Six-phase bcfishpass-reproducing pipeline, driven lnk_config() + lnk_load_overrides(). Every phase reads data table takes cfg (manifest) loaded (named list lnk_load_overrides()). Callers materialize thread loaded . - lnk_pipeline_setup(conn, schema, overwrite) — create per-run working schema. - lnk_pipeline_load(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — crossings + modelled fixes + PSCIS status overrides. Reads loaded$user_modelled_crossing_fixes, loaded$user_pscis_barrier_status, loaded$user_crossings_misc. - lnk_pipeline_prepare(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — falls, definite + control, habitat confirms, gradient barriers, natural_barriers, barrier overrides, per-model minimal reduction, base segments. Manifest-key gating via loaded$user_barriers_definite_control loaded$user_habitat_classification (DB probes). - lnk_pipeline_break(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — sequential frs_break_apply config-defined order: observations → gradient minimal → barriers_definite (separate break source) → habitat endpoints → crossings. - lnk_pipeline_classify(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — assembles fresh.streams_breaks (gradient FULL + falls + barriers_definite + crossings, WSG-filtered) runs frs_habitat_classify(). barriers_definite enters directly bcfishpass appends user-definite post-filter (via observation override). - lnk_pipeline_connect(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — per-species cluster + connected_waterbody. - lnk_pipeline_species(cfg, loaded, aoi) — canonical helper “species config classifies AOI” (intersects cfg$species loaded$wsg_species_presence presence; falls back loaded$parameters_fresh$species_code cfg$species missing).

title: link
text: Issues, PR descriptions, commit messages client-visible deliverables, internal notes. Avoid artifacts: - Framing work unsolicited unpaid (“assigned client”) - Self-justifying adjectives (“defensible”, “rigorous”) — show, don’t claim - Internal workflow meta (PWF refs, SRED xrefs, planning context) - Performative effort language (“attempts unsuccessful”) — state factual current state Integrity-preserving ≠ self-effacing. Factual, performatively humble. Scope: repo artifacts (issues, PRs, commits, reports). apply internal planning docs, CLAUDE.md, chat. Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

code: # --- "Two barriers are both high severity. #      One blocks 0.3km of rearing habitat. #      The other blocks 12km of spawning habitat for chinook. #      Which do you fix first?" --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Score crossings lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Compute upstream habitat from fresh output lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat") # Added spawning_km, rearing_km to working.crossings # Summary: #   spawning_km: min=0.0, median=2.3, max=45.1 #   rearing_km:  min=0.0, median=5.7, max=89.2  # Now you have severity + habitat — the full picture # ORDER BY severity DESC, spawning_km DESC # to find high-severity barriers blocking the most habitat.  # --- Custom metrics --- lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat",   cols_sum = c(spawning_km = "spawning",                rearing_km = "rearing",                lake_ha = "lake_rearing")) } # }

title: Build barrier override list from evidence sources — lnk_barrier_overrides
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. barriers Character. Schema-qualified table barriers columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode_ltree, localcode_ltree, label. Typically fresh.streams_breaks. observations Character NULL. Schema-qualified table fish observations columns: species_code, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode, localcode, observation_date. Typically bcfishobs.observations. habitat Character NULL. Schema-qualified table confirmed habitat canonical shape (post-2026-04-26 bcfishpass): one row per (segment x species) columns species_code, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, upstream_route_measure, plus per-habitat-type indicator columns spawning rearing (integer 1 / NULL text 't'/'true'). confirmed habitat (spawning rearing flagged) upstream barrier removes (threshold = 1). exclusions Character NULL. Schema-qualified table observation exclusions column fish_observation_point_id. Flagged observations removed counting. control Character NULL. Schema-qualified table barrier controls columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, barrier_ind. Barriers table barrier_ind = TRUE overridden observations — species params$observation_control_apply TRUE. Resident species routinely inhabit reaches upstream anadromous-blocking falls (post-glacial connectivity, ocean-return requirement), observations still count unless flag says otherwise. Habitat confirmations (habitat argument) higher-trust observations — bypass control table entirely, species. params Data frame per-species parameters. Must columns: species_code, observation_threshold, observation_date_min, observation_buffer_m, observation_species. Optional column observation_control_apply (logical) — TRUE, control table blocks overrides species; FALSE/NA/missing, species ignores control. Bcfishpass defaults: TRUE CH/CM/CO/PK/SK/ST, FALSE BT/WCT. See configs/bcfishpass/parameters_fresh.csv. cols_index Character vector. Column names index barriers table fwa_upstream() performance. Indexes created EXISTS. Default c("blue_line_key", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree") — columns exist table indexed. Character. Schema-qualified output table name. verbose Logical. Report counts.

what: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: Recomputes sha256 byte shape checksums every file declared bundle's provenance: block compares recorded values. Returns tibble expected vs observed; flags drift axis separately.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
code: lnk_config_verify(cfg, strict = FALSE)

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: cfg lnk_config object lnk_config(). strict Logical. TRUE, errors file drifted either axis. Default FALSE warns returns tibble inspection.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: tibble columns: file — path relative cfg$dir byte_expected — byte checksum recorded manifest byte_observed — byte checksum recomputed current file byte_drift — logical, TRUE byte checksums differ shape_expected — shape checksum recorded manifest, NA manifest shape_checksum field shape_observed — shape checksum recomputed current file's header line shape_drift — logical, TRUE shape checksums differ (manifest shape_expected compare ) missing — logical, TRUE file longer exists disk (observed values NA) tibble carries one row per provenanced file. bundle provenance: block (cfg$provenance NULL) returns empty tibble columns.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: Byte drift (byte_drift) — file content changed (rows added/edited/removed, whole-file re-shape). Detected via sha256 full file. Catches every kind change tell kind. Shape drift (shape_drift) — file's header changed (column added / renamed / removed / reshaped). Detected via sha256 first line file (whitespace-normalized). pure-value change (rows added column change) shows byte_drift = TRUE shape_drift = FALSE. column rename shows TRUE. Header-fingerprint catches dominant failure mode (column structure change); type changes within stable columns detected — require value-level inspection scope . Use run time detect silent drift — file edited without re-recording checksum, external CSV re-synced path. Drift two pipeline runs DB state package versions almost always traces back config-file edit; lnk_config_verify() fastest way localize change.

what: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
text: Opens connection PostgreSQL database containing fwapg, bcfishpass, bcfishobs. Defaults PG_*_SHARE environment variables used fresh fpr (Docker-hosted fwapg). Falls back standard PG* variables local PostgreSQL.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
code: lnk_db_conn(   dbname = Sys.getenv("PG_DB_SHARE", Sys.getenv("PGDATABASE", "postgis")),   host = Sys.getenv("PG_HOST_SHARE", Sys.getenv("PGHOST", "localhost")),   port = as.integer(Sys.getenv("PG_PORT_SHARE", Sys.getenv("PGPORT", "5432"))),   user = Sys.getenv("PG_USER_SHARE", Sys.getenv("PGUSER", "postgres")),   password = Sys.getenv("PG_PASS_SHARE", Sys.getenv("PGPASSWORD", "")) )

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
text: dbname Database name. Defaults PG_DB_SHARE PGDATABASE. host Host. Defaults PG_HOST_SHARE PGHOST. port Port. Defaults PG_PORT_SHARE PGPORT. user User. Defaults PG_USER_SHARE PGUSER. password Password. Defaults PG_PASS_SHARE PGPASSWORD.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
text: DBI::DBIConnection object.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
text: Checks PG_*_SHARE first (Docker fwapg convention shared fresh::frs_db_conn()), standard PostgreSQL variables (PGHOST, etc.). means lnk_db_conn() works identically frs_db_conn() packages connect database.

title: Connect to FWA PostgreSQL database — lnk_db_conn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default — reads PG_*_SHARE env vars (Docker fwapg) conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Override for a specific database conn <- lnk_db_conn(dbname = "fishpass", host = "db.example.com")  # Use with other lnk_* functions conn <- lnk_db_conn() lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  DBI::dbDisconnect(conn) } # }

what: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: Read one correction CSVs, validate structure, write PostgreSQL table. step one override pipeline: load -> lnk_override_validate() -> lnk_override_apply().

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
code: lnk_load(   conn,   csv,   to,   cols_id = "modelled_crossing_id",   cols_required = NULL,   cols_provenance = c("reviewer", "review_date", "source"),   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. csv Character. Path CSV file, vector paths load multiple files table (e.g., one per field season). Character. Schema-qualified destination table name (e.g., "working.overrides_modelled"). cols_id Character. Column(s) used crossing identifier. System-agnostic — "stream_crossing_id", "chris_culvert_id", ID system uses. cols_required Character vector. Columns must exist every CSV. Fails fast informative error naming missing column file. cols_id always required need repeated . cols_provenance Character vector. Provenance columns track reviewed . Kept present CSV, silently skipped absent. Set NULL disable provenance tracking. overwrite Logical. TRUE (default), drop recreate table. FALSE, append existing table.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: destination table name (invisibly), piping lnk_override_validate() lnk_override_apply().

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: Override CSVs represent hand-reviewed crossing corrections accumulated across field seasons imagery review. row says "crossing's attribute changed value." Fail fast: structure validated data written. Missing required columns produce clear error naming column file path. Multi-file load: pass vector CSV paths combine overrides different sources (field seasons, watersheds, reviewers). first file creates table; subsequent files append. Provenance optional: teams mature QA processes track reviewer, review_date, source. New projects can start without columns add later.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
code: # --- What does an override CSV look like? --- csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "overrides_example.csv", package = "link") overrides <- read.csv(csv_path) print(overrides) #>   modelled_crossing_id barrier_result_code  reviewer review_date         source #> 1                 1001            PASSABLE  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 4                 1004            PASSABLE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 5                 1005             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-10-01 imagery review #   modelled_crossing_id barrier_result_code  reviewer review_date        source # 1                 1001            PASSABLE  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20   field visit # ... # Each row corrects one crossing. The reviewer and date tell you # who made the call and when — your audit trail.  # --- Load into database (the typical workflow) --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Single file — most common case lnk_load(conn,   csv  = "data/overrides/modelled_xings_fixes.csv",   to   = "working.overrides_modelled",   cols_required = c("barrier_result_code"))  # Multiple files from different field seasons lnk_load(conn,   csv  = c("data/overrides/2024_field.csv",            "data/overrides/2025_field.csv"),   to   = "working.overrides_modelled")  # Then validate and apply: lnk_override_validate(conn, "working.overrides_modelled",   "working.crossings") lnk_override_apply(conn, "working.crossings",   "working.overrides_modelled") } # }

what: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: Link crossing records different sources using network position (blue_line_key + downstream_route_measure) within distance tolerance. Bidirectional 1:1 dedup ensures record matches side.

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
code: lnk_match(   conn,   sources,   xref_csv = NULL,   col_blk = "blue_line_key",   col_measure = "downstream_route_measure",   distance = 100,   to = "working.matched_crossings",   overwrite = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. sources List source specs. spec named list : table (required) Schema-qualified table name. col_id (required) ID column source. (optional) Raw SQL filter. Developer API — applied within subquery. col_blk (optional) Override network key column. col_measure (optional) Override measure column. xref_csv Character. Optional path CSV known matches. Must two columns matching col_id first two sources. Applied first — matched IDs excluded spatial matching. col_blk Character. Default network key column. col_measure Character. Default measure column. distance Numeric. Maximum network distance (metres) match. Character. Output table name. overwrite Logical. Overwrite output table exists. verbose Logical. Report match counts.

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: output table name (invisibly). table contains columns: source_a, id_a, source_b, id_b, distance_m.

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: N-way matching: two sources produce pairwise comparisons. 1:1 dedup: two-pass DISTINCT keeps closest match per record sides. many--many inflation. xref priority: xref_csv provided, known matches applied first (distance = 0). Already-matched IDs excluded spatial matching.

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Two-source match lnk_match(conn,   sources = list(     list(table = "whse_fish.pscis_assessment_svw",          col_id = "stream_crossing_id"),     list(table = "bcfishpass.modelled_stream_crossings",          col_id = "modelled_crossing_id")),   to = "working.matched_crossings")  # With hand-curated xref corrections lnk_match(conn,   sources = list(     list(table = "working.pscis", col_id = "stream_crossing_id"),     list(table = "working.crossings", col_id = "modelled_crossing_id")),   xref_csv = "data/overrides/pscis_modelled_xref.csv",   to = "working.matched") } # }

what: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
title: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
text: Check referential integrity (orphans, duplicates) update matching rows. Combines validation application one call.

title: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
code: lnk_override(   conn,   crossings,   overrides,   col_id = "modelled_crossing_id",   cols_update = NULL,   cols_provenance = c("reviewer", "review_date", "reviewer_name", "source"),   validate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. crossings Character. Schema-qualified table update. overrides Character. Schema-qualified override table (output lnk_load()). col_id Character. Join column shared tables. cols_update Character vector. Columns copy overrides crossings. NULL (default) auto-detects: columns tables excluding col_id cols_provenance. cols_provenance Character vector. Columns exclude auto-detection (provenance tracking, data). validate Logical. Run referential integrity check applying. Reports orphans duplicates. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Report validation results update counts.

title: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
text: list n_updated, cols_updated, validate = TRUE, orphans, duplicates, valid_count, total_count. Returned invisibly.

title: Validate and apply overrides to a table — lnk_override
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Load corrections lnk_load(conn,   csv = "data/overrides/modelled_xings_fixes.csv",   to  = "working.fixes",   cols_id = "modelled_crossing_id")  # Validate and apply in one step lnk_override(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   overrides = "working.fixes") # Override validation: working.fixes vs working.crossings #   Total overrides:  947 #   Valid (matched):  940 #   Orphans:            7 #   Duplicates:         0 # Updated 940 of 3597 crossings (barrier_status) } # }

what: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
text: Fourth phase habitat classification pipeline. Builds remaining break-source tables (observations, habitat endpoints, crossings) depend AOI- config-specific data, runs fresh::frs_break_apply() sequentially break sources order defined config. round, id_segment reassigned downstream rounds see contiguous integer IDs.

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
code: lnk_pipeline_break(   conn,   aoi,   cfg,   loaded,   schema,   observations = "bcfishobs.observations" )

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. aoi Character. Watershed group code (today; extends spatial filters later). cfg lnk_config object lnk_config(). loaded Named list tibbles lnk_load_overrides(). Carries observation_exclusions wsg_species_presence. schema Character. Working schema name. observations Character. Schema-qualified observations table (default "bcfishobs.observations").

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
text: conn invisibly, pipe chaining.

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
text: break-source order matters. bcfishpass processes: observations → gradient_minimal → barriers_definite → habitat_endpoints → crossings. order encoded bundled bcfishpass config (cfg$pipeline$break_order) default config specify one.

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
text: Valid entries cfg$pipeline$break_order. Omit entry skip segmentation access-gating source. Bcfishpass-bundle config opts subsurfaceflow parity bcfishpass natural access. Default-bundle leaves pending NewGraph methodology decision. Writes (caller's working schema unless noted): <schema>.observations_breaks — WSG- species-filtered observation positions, data-error exclusions applied <schema>.habitat_endpoints — DRM URM habitat classification table (matches bcfishpass convention) <schema>.crossings_breaks — crossing positions Mutates fresh.streams place — adds segment boundaries break source position, reassigns id_segment

title: Segment the Stream Network at Configured Break Positions — lnk_pipeline_break
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn   <- lnk_db_conn() cfg    <- lnk_config("bcfishpass") loaded <- lnk_load_overrides(cfg) schema <- "working_bulk"  lnk_pipeline_setup(conn, schema) lnk_pipeline_load(conn, "BULK", cfg, loaded, schema) lnk_pipeline_prepare(conn, "BULK", cfg, loaded, schema) lnk_pipeline_break(conn, "BULK", cfg, loaded, schema)  DBI::dbGetQuery(conn,   "SELECT count(*) FROM fresh.streams")  DBI::dbDisconnect(conn) } # }

title: Classify Stream Segments into Habitat per Species — lnk_pipeline_classify
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. aoi Character. Watershed group code (today; extends spatial filters later). cfg lnk_config object lnk_config(). loaded Named list tibbles lnk_load_overrides(). text: conn invisibly, pipe chaining.

title: Classify Stream Segments into Habitat per Species — lnk_pipeline_classify
text: access-gating break table (fresh.streams_breaks) assembled FULL gradient barrier set (minimal one used segmentation), falls, user-identified definite barriers, crossings AOI-filtered ltree values attached. Filtering AOI keeps O(segments × breaks) access-gating join tractable. title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: Thresholds control lnk_score_severity() classifies crossings. default values reflect BC provincial fish passage assessment criteria: High severity outlet_drop >= 0.6m slope_length >= 120 — impassable species flows Moderate severity outlet_drop >= 0.3m slope_length >= 60 — flow-dependent, potentially passable migration flows Low severity everything else crossing present System-agnostic: metric names user-defined strings, hardcoded provincial data system. New Zealand user might define high = list(perch_height = 0.5, pipe_gradient = 0.05). Merge order: code defaults < CSV values < inline arguments. lets ship project CSV still tweak one threshold inline.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
code: # Default BC thresholds — zero config th <- lnk_thresholds() th$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.6 # [1] 0.6  # Override from bundled CSV (same result, shows the format) csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") th_csv <- lnk_thresholds(csv = csv_path) identical(th, th_csv) #> [1] TRUE  # Project-specific: bull trout tolerate higher drops th_bt <- lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8)) th_bt$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.8 # [1] 0.8 — inline override wins  # How thresholds plug into scoring (the integration point) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn() lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings",   thresholds = lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8))) } # }
